titleStmt.title: SII 9.222: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 222. (A.R. No. 678 of 1922.) ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE KOLLAM-BHARATI TEMPLE NEAR KOTTAPALLI, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.

summary.p: The slab is cut off and a part of it is lost. The inscription has no date but refers itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya king Tribhuvanamalladēva. It mentions his feudatory Bijjaṇa-Chōḷa-Mahārāja, who is stated to have been a descendant of king Karikāla, who employed his feudatory chiefs in constructing the banks of the Kāvērī river.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . samasta bhuvanāśraya śrīprithvīvallabha mahā
rājādhirāja paramēśvara paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśrayaku
ḷatilakaṁ cālukyābharaṇaṁ śrīmattribhuvanamalladē
vara vijayarājyamuttarōttarābhivriddhipravarddhamā
namācaṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ saluttamire . svasti caraṇa
sarōruhavihitaviḷōcanatriḷōcanapramukhākhiḷapri
thvīsvarakāritakāvērītīra karikālakularatnapradīpā
kumārāṁkusaṁ śrīmat bijjaṇacōḷama
hārājaru svastyanavarataparamaka
lyāṇābhyudaye sahasraphaḷabhōga
dvitīyalakṣmīsamāne
late The stone is chipped off after this.
Digital edition of SII 9.222 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
